accurate.why: All claims align with the bill text and contextual statutory references (50 U.S.C. 1546a, ISAAECA 1976) are verifiably correct.

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated or contradictory claims; statutory and procedural context is accurate.

states_purpose.why: Clearly states the resolution directs removal of U.S. forces from unauthorized hostilities with Iran.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Correctly invokes the War Powers framework and expedited procedures under 50 U.S.C. 1546a and ISAAECA, though the resolution does not itself amend law.

exceptions_conditions.why: Accurately captures the rule-of-construction carve-outs for self-defense, intelligence sharing, and defensive assistance to allies.

effective_dates.why: The bill specifies no effective date; the summary correctly notes this.

major_provisions_covered.why: Captures the operative removal directive, the unless-authorized condition, and the rule of construction.

objective_tone.why: Neutral presentation with no advocacy or editorializing, including the contentious findings.

coherent.why: Well-organized and logically structured from purpose through findings, operative provision, and carve-outs.

concise.why: Compact with no padding or repetition.

correct_entities.why: Correctly names Mrs. Gillibrand, the Committee on Foreign Relations, the cited statutes, and Operation Epic Fury.

correct_figures.why: The few figures referenced (Feb. 28, 2026 date) are correct and consistent with the bill.
